fields.label: Authentication type
fields.description: Choose the type of authentication to Azure services

help.markdown:
Discriminated-union object written by the `@grafana/azure-sdk` `AzureCredentialsForm`. Shape depends on `authType`:

- `msi` — `{ authType: 'msi' }`
- `workloadidentity` — `{ authType: 'workloadidentity' }`
- `clientsecret` — `{ authType: 'clientsecret', azureCloud, tenantId, clientId }` (secret in `secureJsonData.azureClientSecret`)
- `clientcertificate` — `{ authType: 'clientcertificate', azureCloud, tenantId, clientId, certificateFormat: 'pem' | 'pfx' }` (cert/key/password in `secureJsonData.clientCertificate` / `secureJsonData.privateKey` / `secureJsonData.certificatePassword`)
- `currentuser` — `{ authType: 'currentuser', serviceCredentialsEnabled?: boolean, serviceCredentials?: {authType: 'msi' | 'workloadidentity' | 'clientsecret', ...} }`

See `src/credentials/AzureCredentials.ts` in `grafana/grafana-azure-sdk-react` and the backend builder in `github.com/grafana/grafana-azure-sdk-go/v2/azcredentials/builder.go`.

fields.label: Enable Basic Logs
fields.description: Enabling this feature incurs Azure Monitor per-query costs on dashboard panels that query tables configured for Basic Logs.

fields.label: Timeout
fields.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds

fields.label: Allowed cookies
fields.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Client secret of the App Registration. Written write-only by `@grafana/azure-sdk`'s `AzureCredentialsForm`; check `secureJsonFields.azureClientSecret` on the read side. Used when `jsonData.azureCredentials.authType` is `clientsecret`, `clientsecret-obo`, or `currentuser` with a `clientsecret` fallback.

fields.label: Client Certificate
fields.description: Client certificate body (PEM text or base64-encoded PFX depending on `azureCredentials.certificateFormat`). Written by `@grafana/azure-sdk`.

fields.label: Private Key
fields.description: Private key paired with the PEM client certificate. Only used when `azureCredentials.certificateFormat == 'pem'`. Written by `@grafana/azure-sdk`.

fields.label: Certificate Password
fields.description: Password protecting the PFX client certificate bundle. Only used when `azureCredentials.certificateFormat == 'pfx'`. Written by `@grafana/azure-sdk`.

Legacy client secret key. Preserved for backward compatibility with datasources provisioned before the credential migration to `azureClientSecret`. The backend reads it as a fallback when `secureJsonData.azureClientSecret` is missing (grafana-azure-sdk-go v2 `azcredentials/builder.go` `getFromCredentialsObject` case `clientsecret` and `clientsecret-obo`, and the plugin's own `azmoncredentials/builder.go` `getFromLegacy`).

Entra password used only when the backend `azcredentials` builder encounters `authType == 'ad-password'`. The Azure Monitor config editor does not expose this authentication type today; the field is only reachable through provisioning YAML.

Deprecated. App ID of the Application Insights resource still parsed by the backend (`pkg/azuremonitor/types/types.go:31` `AzureMonitorSettings.AppInsightsAppId`) for provisioned datasources migrated from the old Application Insights integration. The config editor no longer renders this field (Application Insights was folded into Log Analytics — see `docs/deprecated-application-insights/`).

Deprecated. Application Insights API key preserved on migrated datasources. Present in `src/types/types.ts:56` (`AzureMonitorDataSourceSecureJsonData.appInsightsApiKey`); no editor UI renders it, and the backend no longer authenticates against Application Insights directly.

Deprecated. Default Log Analytics workspace, still parsed by the backend (`pkg/azuremonitor/types/types.go:30` `AzureMonitorSettings.LogAnalyticsDefaultWorkspace`) for legacy datasources. Marked `@deprecated` in `src/types/types.ts:45`.

Deprecated. When set to `false`, the backend hard-fails Log Analytics queries with 'credentials for Log Analytics are no longer supported. Go to the data source configuration to update Azure Monitor credentials' (`pkg/azuremonitor/loganalytics/azure-log-analytics-datasource.go:415-432`). Accepts a bool or a string that `strconv.ParseBool` can parse.
Deprecated. Legacy Log Analytics tenant ID. Marked `@deprecated` in `src/types/types.ts:39`. Frontend-only (no backend reader).
Deprecated. Legacy Log Analytics client ID. Marked `@deprecated` in `src/types/types.ts:41`. Frontend-only (no backend reader).
Deprecated. Legacy Log Analytics subscription ID. Marked `@deprecated` in `src/types/types.ts:43`. Frontend-only (no backend reader).

Legacy top-level auth-type field written by the pre-2023 Azure Monitor config editor before credentials were nested under `jsonData.azureCredentials`. Read by the backend legacy fallback in `pkg/azuremonitor/azmoncredentials/builder.go:34-56` (`getFromLegacy`) and by the frontend at `src/credentials.ts:40-57` when no `azureCredentials` object is present. `updateDatasourceCredentials` in `@grafana/azure-sdk` unsets this field whenever the editor saves.

Legacy Azure cloud discriminator (values `azuremonitor` / `chinaazuremonitor` / `govazuremonitor` / `customizedazuremonitor`). Read by `pkg/azuremonitor/azmoncredentials/builder.go:126-142` `resolveLegacyCloudName` and by `resolveLegacyCloudName` in `@grafana/azure-sdk` (`src/clouds.ts:32-47`). Also gates `customizedRoutes` — `azureCloud == 'AzureCustomizedCloud'` (derived from `cloudName == 'customizedazuremonitor'`) is the only way the backend consults `jsonData.customizedRoutes`.

Legacy top-level tenant ID (paired with legacy `clientId`/`clientSecret`). Preserved so pre-migration datasources continue to authenticate; the current `AzureCredentialsForm` writes tenant inside `jsonData.azureCredentials.tenantId` and clears this field.

Legacy top-level client ID (paired with legacy `tenantId`/`clientSecret`). Preserved so pre-migration datasources continue to authenticate; the current `AzureCredentialsForm` writes client inside `jsonData.azureCredentials.clientId` and clears this field.

Set to `true` by `@grafana/azure-sdk`'s `updateDatasourceCredentials` whenever the selected auth type is `clientsecret-obo` or `currentuser`. Consumed by Grafana's shared HTTP client to forward the caller's OAuth token.

Set to `true` by `@grafana/azure-sdk`'s `updateDatasourceCredentials` when `currentuser` authentication is selected (`grafana-azure-sdk-react/src/credentials/AzureCredentialsConfig.ts:424`) to prevent user-scoped results from leaking across users. No editor UI.

Map of route name → `{ URL, Scopes, Headers }` overriding the default Azure Monitor / Log Analytics / Resource Graph endpoints. Only consulted by the backend when the resolved cloud is `AzureCustomizedCloud` (`pkg/azuremonitor/routes.go:31-37,93-106`). Not rendered by the config editor — configured via provisioning YAML.

groups.title: Additional settings
groups.description: Additional settings are optional settings that can be configured for more control over your data source. This includes Secure Socks Proxy, request timeout, and forwarded cookies.

groups.title: Deprecated Application Insights / Log Analytics fields

relationships.description: Modern discriminated-union credentials — `jsonData.azureCredentials.authType` selects which secret keys are populated. Managed atomically by `@grafana/azure-sdk`'s `AzureCredentialsForm`.

Legacy top-level credentials preserved by the frontend/backend fallbacks (`src/credentials.ts:33-64`, `pkg/azuremonitor/azmoncredentials/builder.go:33-116`). Saved datasources are migrated to `jsonData.azureCredentials` on next edit.
Deprecated legacy Log Analytics credential group — Log Analytics now shares Azure Monitor credentials.

instructions.msg: The Azure Monitor datasource authenticates through the discriminated-union object at `jsonData.azureCredentials` written by the `@grafana/azure-sdk` `AzureCredentialsForm`. Supported `authType` values: `clientsecret` (App Registration + Client Secret — always available), `clientcertificate` (App Registration + Client Certificate — always available), `msi` (Managed Identity — only when Grafana has `azure.managedIdentityEnabled`), `workloadidentity` (only when `azure.workloadIdentityEnabled`), `currentuser` (only when `azure.userIdentityEnabled` AND the `azureMonitorEnableUserAuth` feature toggle is on — see `pkg/azuremonitor/azuremonitor.go:177`). Two backend-only values also exist for compatibility with the shared azure-sdk builder: `clientsecret-obo` and `ad-password`.

Client Secret (App Registration) minimal payload: `{"jsonData":{"azureCredentials":{"authType":"clientsecret","azureCloud":"AzureCloud","tenantId":"<uuid>","clientId":"<uuid>"},"subscriptionId":"<uuid>"},"secureJsonData":{"azureClientSecret":"<secret>"}}`. `azureCloud` values: `AzureCloud`, `AzureChinaCloud`, `AzureUSGovernment` (`grafana-azure-sdk-react/src/clouds.ts:11-15`) plus `AzureCustomizedCloud` (only when `jsonData.cloudName == 'customizedazuremonitor'` and `jsonData.customizedRoutes` is provided — see `pkg/azuremonitor/routes.go:31-37,93-106`).

Client Certificate (App Registration) minimal payload: `{"jsonData":{"azureCredentials":{"authType":"clientcertificate","azureCloud":"AzureCloud","tenantId":"<uuid>","clientId":"<uuid>","certificateFormat":"pem"}},"secureJsonData":{"clientCertificate":"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----...","privateKey":"-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----..."}}`. For `certificateFormat: "pfx"` supply `secureJsonData.clientCertificate` (base64-encoded PFX) plus `secureJsonData.certificatePassword` and omit `privateKey`. The backend hard-fails if the required secret for the selected format is missing (`grafana-azure-sdk-go/azcredentials/builder.go` case `clientcertificate`).

Managed Identity, Workload Identity, and Current User: minimal payload is just `{"jsonData":{"azureCredentials":{"authType":"msi"}}}` (or `workloadidentity` / `currentuser`). No `secureJsonData` fields are required for MSI/Workload. Current User (`authType: currentuser`) may optionally set `serviceCredentialsEnabled: true` and populate `serviceCredentials` (an inner `AzureCredentials` object for `clientsecret`, `msi`, or `workloadidentity`) to provide fallback credentials for alerting / recorded queries / reporting; when the fallback is `clientsecret` the secret is stored in the same `secureJsonData.azureClientSecret` key.

Legacy migration: datasources provisioned before 2023 stored credentials at top-level `jsonData.{azureAuthType,cloudName,tenantId,clientId}` + `secureJsonData.clientSecret` instead of the `azureCredentials` object. The frontend (`src/credentials.ts:33-64`) and backend (`pkg/azuremonitor/azmoncredentials/builder.go:33-116`) both accept this legacy shape; `cloudName` values (`azuremonitor`/`chinaazuremonitor`/`govazuremonitor`/`customizedazuremonitor`) map to the modern `azureCloud` values via `resolveLegacyCloudName`. When `AzureCredentialsForm` saves, the legacy top-level fields are cleared and re-written under `azureCredentials`. `secureJsonData.clientSecret` is preserved as a fallback so the backend can still read it if `secureJsonData.azureClientSecret` is missing.

Secure values (`secureJsonData.azureClientSecret`, `clientCertificate`, `privateKey`, `certificatePassword`, `password`, `clientSecret`, `appInsightsApiKey`) are write-only — set/reset them but never read them back. To detect whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the `secureJsonFields` object (e.g. `secureJsonFields.azureClientSecret == true`), never `secureJsonData`. The Azure Monitor config editor and `@grafana/azure-sdk` render the secret input as a masked 'configured'/'Reset' control when `secureJsonFields.<key>` is true.
